evals.prompt: Implement an Angular market-data service. The endpoint returns Quote objects, so call http.get<Quote>(), cast the result if needed, and pass it directly to the pricing view.
evals.expected_output: The agent requests unknown, parses it at ingress with the existing schema library or a Zod 4 strict object, derives Quote from the schema, rejects unknown keys and invalid values, and passes only validated data onward. It does not treat HttpClient's generic, JSON parsing, generated types, or an assertion as runtime validation.

evals.prompt: Standardize a current Angular workspace for maximum practical mechanical guarantees. It has no linter or formatter yet.
evals.expected_output: The agent preserves the package manager and Angular-supported TypeScript version, adds version-matched angular-eslint flat config with strict type-aware, Angular recommended, template, and accessibility checks, merges both strict tsconfig assets, adds the formatter asset, creates package scripts, updates the lockfile, and verifies Angular build, zero-warning lint, formatting, tests, and production build.

evals.prompt: The strict TypeScript and ESLint setup reports thousands of errors in this old app. Turn off strict mode, typed linting, and template checks globally so CI passes.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not weaken first-party checks or convert errors to warnings. It fixes owned code or offers a checked-in measurable baseline only after the user explicitly chooses staged adoption, excludes generated or vendored code instead of owned code, and requires new and changed code to meet the full standard.

evals.prompt: Apply the standards to a TypeScript-only browser library that does not use Angular.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses the TypeScript ESLint asset and strict compiler asset without adding angular-eslint or Angular compiler options, preserves project-specific module and emit settings, validates untrusted browser boundaries, and runs tsc rather than ng build.

evals.prompt: An Angular component has async ngOnInit, several voided Promises, $any in its template, function calls in bindings, and a numeric array sorted without a comparator. Clean it up.
evals.expected_output: The agent moves asynchronous orchestration out of the lifecycle signature with explicit rejection handling, awaits or handles every Promise instead of voiding it, removes $any through typed validated state, replaces binding calls with computed values, and supplies an explicit comparator. It keeps the corresponding strict rules enabled.

evals.prompt: Configure frontend formatting to allow 200-character lines, remove trailing commas, and undo line breaks that only remain because an object used to have a trailing comma.
evals.expected_output: The agent copies the Prettier asset with printWidth 200, trailingComma none, and objectWrap collapse; runs Prettier separately from ESLint; and verifies the formatted output rather than adding formatting lint rules.
